introSection: The Great Lego Race is a steel wild mouse roller coaster located at Legoland Florida in Winter Haven, Florida, USA. The ride was relocated from Legoland Windsor, where it was called Jungle Coaster.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride originally opened at Legoland Windsor in March 2004 under the name of Jungle Coaster. However, it was removed in 2009 due to noise complaints from local residents and replaced with the Legoland Hotel in 2012.

The ride was then relocated to its present location at Legoland Florida, where it opened with the park on October 15, 2011 as Project X.

On March 20, 2018 the ride was renamed from Project X to The Great Lego Race. A virtual reality option with VR headsets was offered at this time.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride is a stock model from Mack Rides known as Large Park Wild Mouse.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The cars are designed to look like they were built using Lego Technic bricks. When the ride was located at Legoland Windsor, the cars had canopies over them to make them resemble 4x4s.

fullText:
The Great Lego Race is a steel wild mouse roller coaster located at Legoland Florida in Winter Haven, Florida, USA. The ride was relocated from Legoland Windsor, where it was called Jungle Coaster.

The ride originally opened at Legoland Windsor in March 2004 under the name of Jungle Coaster. However, it was removed in 2009 due to noise complaints from local residents and replaced with the Legoland Hotel in 2012.

The ride was then relocated to its present location at Legoland Florida, where it opened with the park on October 15, 2011 as Project X.

On March 20, 2018 the ride was renamed from Project X to The Great Lego Race. A virtual reality option with VR headsets was offered at this time.

The ride is a stock model from Mack Rides known as Large Park Wild Mouse.

The cars are designed to look like they were built using Lego Technic bricks. When the ride was located at Legoland Windsor, the cars had canopies over them to make them resemble 4x4s.
